info.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM - Explore Goals
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

post.summary: Find a development goal to explore
post.description: Finds library or shared development goals that the employee can explore.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

requestBody.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.

offset.description: Defines the starting position of the resource collection. If the offset exceeds the resource count, then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

pPersonId.description: Unique person identifier of the employee whose available library and shared development goals need to be found.

limit.description: Restriction for the number of development goals for exploring that's returned. If the limit exceeds the resource count, then the framework returns only the available development goals for exploring.

pAssignmentId.description: Unique identifier for the assignment of the employee whose available library and shared development goals need to be found.

pParentFlow.description: Identifies the role context for finding development goals that the employee can explore. The role context can be worker, manager, or HR specialist.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

result.description: Library or shared development goals that the employee can explore.
Library or shared development goals that the employee can explore.

parameters.name: PersonId
parameters.description: The value of this parameter could be a hash of the key that is used to uniquely identify the resource item. The client should not generate the hash key value.  Instead, the client should query on the collection resource with a filter to navigate to a specific resource item. For example: products?q=InventoryItemId=<value1>

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are fields that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The fields specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the fields will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency fields. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

post.summary: Find a performance goal to explore
post.description: Finds library or shared performance goals that the employee can explore.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

requestBody.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.

offset.description: Defines the starting position of the resource collection. If the offset exceeds the resource count, then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

pPersonId.description: Unique person identifier of the employee whose available library and shared performance goals need to be found.

limit.description: Restriction for the number of performance goals for exploring that's returned. If the limit exceeds the resource count, then the framework returns only the available performance goals for exploring.

pAssignmentId.description: Unique identifier for the assignment of the employee whose available library and shared performance goals need to be found.

pParentFlow.description: Identifies the role context for finding performance goals that the employee can explore. The role context can be worker, manager, or HR specialist.

pReviewPeriodId.description: Unique identifier for the review period for which the performance goals that the employee can explore need to be found.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

result.description: Library or shared performance goals that the employee can explore.
Library or shared performance goals that the employee can explore.

post.description: Finds a performance or development goal with the specified goal name that the employee can explore.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

requestBody.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.

flowContext.description: Identifies the role context for finding goals to explore, which can be worker, manager, or HR specialist.

offset.description: Defines the starting position of the resource collection. If the offset exceeds the resource count, then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

query.description: Defines the where clause for the search. The resource collection is queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions.

limit.description: Restriction for the number of goals for exploring that's returned. If the limit exceeds the resource count, then the framework returns only the available goals for exploring.

context.description: Identifies the search view context. Valid values are SHARED and LIBRARY. If no context is passed, then both shared and library goals are included in the search result.

searchFields.description: Search fields for the find goals that can be explored keyword query. The search field can be the goal name or the owner name.

personId.description: Unique person identifier of the employee who has to explore goals.

filters.description: Facet filters for the find goals that can be explored search. These filter attributes depend on the view context. If the view context is Library, then the facet filters are business unit, department, job family, goal type, category, and subtype. If the view context is Shared, then the main facet filter is the goal type. For performance goals, the review period can also be used as a facet filter.

sort.description: Sort criteria for the find goals that can be explored search, which is the goal name.

displayFields.description: Display fields to include in the response of the find goals that can be explored search query. The display fields can include fields such as the goal name, description, start date, target completion date, goal type, and the name of the person who has shared the goal.

assignmentId.description: Unique identifier for the assignment of the employee whose available library and shared development goals need to be found.

autocompleteQuery.description: Indicates whether the query is auto completed. Valid values are Y and N. The default value is Y.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

result.description: Goals that the employee can explore.
Goals that the employee can explore.

post.summary: Get performance comments and goal level comments
post.description: Gets the overall section level performance comments and the goal level comments from check-in documents.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

requestBody.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.

payload.description: Identifies whether comments need to be fetched for performance or development goals.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

result.description: Overall section level performance comments and the goal level comments from check-in documents.
Overall section level performance comments and the goal level comments from check-in documents.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter restricts the number of resources returned inside the resource collection. If the limit exceeds the resource count then the framework will only return the available resources.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Used to define the starting position of the resource collection. If offset exceeds the resource count then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

parameters.name: totalResults
parameters.description: The resource collection representation will include the "estimated row count" when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.  The default value is "false".

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
This query parameter defines the where clause. The resource collection will be queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions. Example: ?q=Deptno>=10 and <= 30;Loc!=NY

Format: ?q=expression1;expression2

You can use these queryable attributes to filter this collection resource using the q query parameter:
  • PersonId; string; Unique person identifier of the employee who has to explore goals.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: This parameter orders a resource collection based on the specified fields.  The parameter value is a comma-separated string of attribute names, each optionally followed by a colon and "asc" or "desc".  Specify "asc" for ascending and "desc" for descending. The default value is "asc".  For example, ?orderBy=field1:asc,field2:desc

parameters.name: finder
parameters.description:
Used as a predefined finder to search the collection.

Format ?finder=<finderName>;<variableName>=<variableValue>,<variableName2>=<variableValue2>

The following are the available finder names and corresponding finder variables

  • PrimaryKey Finds all goals that the employee can explore.
    Finder Variables
    • PersonId; string; Unique person identifier of the employee who has to explore goals.

x-adf-finder-formats.name: PrimaryKey
x-adf-finder-formats.description: Finds all goals that the employee can explore.

PersonId.description: Unique person identifier of the employee who has to explore goals.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

exploreGoalAction.description: Ignores the shared performance or development goal of the employee.

findPerformanceComments.description: Gets the overall section level performance comments and the goal level comments from check-in documents.

getDevSharedAndLibraryGoals.description: Finds library or shared development goals that the employee can explore.

getPerfSharedAndLibraryGoals.description: Finds library or shared performance goals that the employee can explore.

findByAdvancedSearchQuery.description: Finds a performance or development goal with the specified goal name that the employee can explore.

post.description: Ignores the shared performance or development goal of the employee.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

requestBody.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.

goalType.description: Identifies the type of goal that can be explored. The goal type can be Performance or Development.

flowContext.description: Identifies the person flow context. Valid values are WORKER, MANAGER, and HR.

pPersonId.description: Unique person identifier of the employee who has to explore goals.

inputParams.description: Additional input parameters required for actions related to goals that can be explored.

pAssignmentId.description: Unique identifier for the assignment of the employee who has to explore goals.

exploreGoalId.description: Unique identifier for the goal that can be explored.

actionContext.description: Identifies the action context for the goal that can be explored. Valid action context is HIDE_SHARED_GOAL.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

result.description: The identifier for the goal to explore and the value of the ViewSharedGoalFlag attribute that indicates if that goal has been ignored. If the value of the ViewSharedGoalFlag attribute is Y, then the goal to explore can be viewed, and if it's N, then the goal to explore is ignored.

The identifier for the goal to explore and the value of the ViewSharedGoalFlag attribute that indicates if that goal has been ignored. If the value of the ViewSharedGoalFlag attribute is Y, then the goal to explore can be viewed, and if it's N, then the goal to explore is ignored.

PersonId.description: Unique person identifier of the employee who has to explore goals.

limit.description: The actual paging size used by the server.
